what_it_does: Renders a phone-like comparison screen showing an earlier take and this take, lists four changes, displays preference choices, a keep action, and a bottom icon rail.
observations: Loads Plus Jakarta Sans, IBM Plex Mono, shared piece and phone styles, and a shared organisms runtime.
Uses inline CSS for take panels, the vertical connector, verdict pills, and local layout details.
Shows two labeled preview panels: earlier take v1 and this take v2.
The current take includes an ochre diagonal band and three colored panels using warm, cool, and nested variables.
Populates four change rows from a local array through innerHTML.
Builds a five-icon rail with DNA.org.icon, with the compass icon highlighted in gold.
standouts: The socket-dash-plug vertical joint visually connects the two takes.
The screen combines miniature previews with a short change ledger and preference controls.
The current take is visually differentiated through color, an ochre label, and a band marker.
conventions: Uses shared design tokens through CSS variables such as --dna-space-20, --line, --gold, and --page.
Uses existing p-* and screen classes for phone-surface structure.
Keeps content and small data arrays directly in the file.
Uses inline style attributes for panel background colors.
concerns: Depends on DNA.org.icon being available from the runtime without a local guard.
The preference and keep controls are static div/span elements rather than semantic form controls.
Static rows are inserted with innerHTML, though the values are local literals.
notes: A self-contained phone mockup for comparing two design takes and recording what changed.
questions: Should the preference pills and Keep This Take action be interactive or only visual in this screen?
Are the four change rows fixed copy for this piece or placeholders for dynamic comparison data?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated compare-screen purpose matches the rendered two-take comparison and change list.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make a design revision legible by pairing before/after miniatures with concise change statements and a selection affordance.
novelty: The plug-and-socket connector between takes gives the comparison a physical handoff metaphor inside a compact mobile layout.
contribution: Contributes a neutral comparison pattern: paired take previews, explicit change rows, preference state, keep action, and bottom navigation within the phone-surface system.
summary_for_embedding: A single HTML phone mockup that compares an earlier design take with a current take, lists four visual/content changes, and shows preference plus keep controls.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for presenting a concise, mobile-form design-revision ledger where a viewer can inspect what changed and mark the preferred take.
